name: Irving Berlin
abstract: Irving Berlin (born Israel Isidore Beilin; May 11, 1888 – September 22, 1989) was a Russian-born American songwriter. His music forms a large part of the Great American Songbook.

about_quotes.speaker: Leon Rene
about_quotes.evidence: My uncle said, you think you're going to be like Irving Berlin? ... I said, maybe so. I don't say I'll be as great as Irving Berlin, but I said, I might use him as my... As a goal

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: Irving Berlin, Richard Rodgers, George Gershwin, that’s what changed jazz. ... the pop songwriters like even the unschooled ones were using the minor seventh like Irving Berlin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Conrad "Connie" Jones
about_quotes.evidence: we had a lot of Irving Berlin sheet music... I came across a copy of Always, that incidentally is autographed. By Irving Berlin.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: Irving Berlin songs — Absolutely, positively. [sings] In the still of the night — that’s just one song that we would play
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: These tunes were, you know, Gershwin and Irving Berlin and these guys, they wrote some pretty songs
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: let alone an Irving Berlin song or Cole Porter or Stephen Foster for that matter.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015
